source_display: AI as Brain Crutches for ADHD
evidence: Vrijen shared his personal interest in building AI tools as 'brain crutches' for ADHD—filling gaps between therapy, medication, and coaching

Vrijen shared advice from his edtech experience: avoid the 'death by a thousand cuts' of endless unpaid pilots. Instead, seek paid pilots with scoped changes and identify design partners willing to work with the product as-is
A cornerstone customer providing reliable revenue creates options—whether to scale, attract others, or seek investment

source_display: PMF Like Obscenity: Know It When You See It
evidence: He defined product-market fit using the Supreme Court's obscenity standard: 'you know it when you see it'—when you have it, you don't think about it; when you don't, you obsess over it constantly
Vrijen emphasized that founder-led sales provides customer research, network building, and messaging refinement—critical activities for early-stage companies
Vrijen outlined a spectrum of community approaches: one-to-many broadcasting → platforms with comment sections → private user research groups → full communities
Vrijen countered that social media only works long-term when done authentically—owning one's style rather than performing
A peer-to-peer strategic conversation between Vrijen Attawar (founder of Careerspan) and Chris Hanson (special education teacher turned edtech founder)
Chris is building an ADHD support product called '360'
Vrijen invited Chris to join the Future of Higher Education group (6,000 members globally, ~1,000 in NYC), where Vrijen serves as NYC co-lead
Positioning: Careerspan as a 'human data company'. Video demo should be 'human data oriented'—show the data collection, then the results
Core message: 'We are a great solution because we collect first-party data'
Key differentiator: opted-in, first-party candidate data. Reference to Eightfold lawsuit—positioning against publicly scraped data risks
Logan's insight: 'Why don't we make a 30-second look at the information we capture via Careerspan?' ... 'A deck is not as impactful as watching it in action'

source_display: Eightfold Lawsuit as Market Timing Hook
evidence: Hook: 'This moment in time is important' (regulatory pressure on third-party data). Reference to Eightfold lawsuit—positioning against publicly scraped data risks
Logan's tasks: Develop email content aligned with video, Build two versions (sourcing vs. internal mobility), Use LinkedIn data export to identify additional prospects
V's tasks: Record 45-60 second demo video showing scan + first-party data, Work with Zoe on tight script, Prep email body copy and contact list
Two distinct video/message versions: 1. Sourcing/Matching/External Hiring — for recruiting teams. 2. Internal Mobility — for enterprise HR/L&D
Internal Mobility targets: Reliance, Uber (scale-driven)
